title: Dirty oil flows to China
summary:
Chart of the Week: Dirty oil flows to China
Upward revision of the monthly volume of dirty oil flows to China since the beginning of September
The third week of October saw a noticeable improvement in market sentiment in the crude oil freight sector. At the same time, we have observed an increase of rates in the clean tanker segment. This upswing can be attributed to the new geopolitical crisis between Iran and Israel, which has led to a significant level of uncertainty in the oil market.

children.title: Chart of the Week: Dirty oil flows to China
section_path: Chart of the Week: Dirty oil flows to China
children.section_path_text: Chart of the Week: Dirty oil flows to China
children.summary:
Upward revision of the monthly volume of dirty oil flows to China since the beginning of September
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-20T121418.840.png | assets/2023-10-20_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-20t121418840_8dc863c69409.png
The third week of October saw a noticeable improvement in market sentiment in the crude oil freight sector. At the same time, we have observed an increase of rates in the clean tanker segment.

children.title: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT
children.summary:
Market Rates (WS)
‘Dirty' WS VLCC - Suezmax - Aframax Firmer
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-20T121655.627.png | assets/2023-10-20_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-20t121655627_5bfe7461630e.png
The crude freight market sentiment posed a significant upturn from the beginning of the third week of October with an increase that seems to hold as we are in a geopolitical spike. VLCC MEG-China freight rates rose above WS 60, up 40% from the previous week and up 67% from a month ago.

children.title: ‘Product' WS LR2 Firmer
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-20T121919.964.png | assets/2023-10-20_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-20t121919964_fd4bbad96e17.png
LR2 AG freight rates rose to 160 WS, up 9% in one month and up 90% from the record low in week 28.

children.title: Panamax Firmer
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-20T122123.881.png | assets/2023-10-20_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-20t122123881_c61989a75a05.png
Panamax Carib-to-USG rates recorded the first increase since the end of week 27, reaching 200 WS, 56% more than a month ago.

children.title: ‘Clean' MR Mixed
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-20T122251.727.png | assets/2023-10-20_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-20t122251727_13699cd83d80.png
MR1 rates for the Baltic continent rose to WS 290, up 60% from a month ago. MR2 rates for shipments from the continent to the U.S.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY
children.summary:
' Dirty' (#vessels) - Mixed
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-20T122547.177.png | assets/2023-10-20_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-20t122547177_b06c9256566e.png
The supply trend for the Ras Tanura VLCC showed a significant downward trend since the end of week 40, while the downward correction for the Suezmax Wafr was even stronger. VLCC Ras Tanura: The number of ships has fallen to 57, 4 below the annual average, with a clear downward trend at the end of week 39.

children.title: 'Clean' LR2 (#vessels) - Decreasing
children.summary: Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-20T122939.524.png | assets/2023-10-20_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-20t122939524_cb84025da925.png

children.title: MR1 (#vessels) - Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-20T123025.133.png | assets/2023-10-20_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-20t123025133_729ae16ccb82.png
Clean LR2 AG Jubail: The number of recorded ships has maintained the previous week's decrease of 4, which is 8 less than the average for the year. Clean MR1 Algeria Skikda: The current count is 36 ships, 9 more than in week 40, while the figure is 2 above the annual average.

children.title: ‘Dirty' Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-20T123315.135.png | assets/2023-10-20_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-20t123315135_9afca7d2d86d.png
Dirty tonne days: The picture remained negative in all crude tanker categories, with a slight increase in Suezmax vessels, but the overall demand outlook continues to weaken.

children.title: ‘Clean' Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-10-20T123413.813.png | assets/2023-10-20_signal-tanker-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2023-10-20t123413813_afd0797dbad0.png
Panamax tonne days: The growth rate has continued to decline since the end of the 39th week, reaching one of the lowest levels since the beginning of this year. Clean MR tonne days: The picture has not changed from the downward correction of the last weeks, while the last peak for the MR1 size was reached almost ten weeks ago.
